      If the JAL-path was the binding fanout for alu_result,
      we save ~0.5-1.0 ns. If the ALU output was binding, no
      change.
    - pc_next mux: similar. The mux shape is unchanged but
      two of its four inputs (the misalign-fallback and the
      sequential-default) drop their +4 adder dependency.

  Expected Fmax delta: 0 to +2-3%. Conservative estimate; the
  win depends on whether the EX/MEM input cone was actually
  Fmax-binding after hyp-008.

IPC delta: ZERO. Same architectural behavior, same retirement
order, same trap conditions, same target values. cycles/iter
remains 4,491,817 / 10 = 449,182.

TESTS:

  No new cocotb tests required. The change is purely value-
  equivalent: every existing test in test_pipeline.py,
  test_decoder.py, test_imm_gen.py, test_reg_file.py, and
  test_alu.py covers the same surface as before, with
  pc_next / alu_result for JAL sourcing from a registered
  value instead of an EX-stage adder. The full riscv-formal
  suite (insn_b{eq,ne,lt,ge,ltu,geu}, insn_jal, insn_jalr,
  pc_fwd_ch0, pc_bwd_ch0, unique, liveness, ill, etc.) proves
  the change does not regress any RVFI invariant under
  symbolic stimulus.

changes.description:
Add ONE 32-bit `pc_plus_4` field at the END of the id_ex_t
packed struct, AFTER the `pc_plus_imm` field added by hyp-008.
Position at the END preserves the existing bit ordering of
all previously-defined fields (the discipline hyp-006/008
documented; violated by hyp-004/005).

Header comment: pc_plus_4 is a value-equivalent
pre-computation of `in.pc + 32'd4`, NOT a control bit. It
replaces three uses of (in.pc + 32'd4) in ex_stage.sv: the
sequential pc_next default, the misalign trap pc_next
override, and the JAL link alu_result. Same discipline as
pc_plus_imm: a value-bearing pipeline field is safe because
it is unconditionally the same sum the EX stage would have
computed and is consumed only when valid=1 (which already
implies the ID/EX register captured a real instruction).
This contrasts with the speculative / contract-bearing bits
(predicted_taken, predicted_jal) that broke hyp-004/005.

Do NOT change if_id_t, ex_mem_t, mem_wb_t, or ctrl_t. No
changes to ALU op codes or branch op codes. The struct
addition is the minimal footprint required to lift one
adder from EX to ID.

Add a combinational signal alongside the existing
`pc_plus_imm_w` precompute (added by hyp-008):

  logic [31:0] pc_plus_4_w;
  always_comb pc_plus_4_w = in.pc + 32'd4;

In the existing always_ff register block, under the
`!(reset || flush) && !stall` capture branch (where
`reg_q.pc_plus_imm <= pc_plus_imm_w;` is already assigned),
add:

  reg_q.pc_plus_4 <= pc_plus_4_w;

On reset/flush the entire reg_q clears to '0 via the bulk
zero-assign, so pc_plus_4 naturally goes to 0 — harmless
because valid=0 also gates downstream consumers. Same
handling as pc_plus_imm.

No other change. Do not touch the decoder instantiation,
the imm_gen instantiation, the regfile read addresses, or
the hazard inputs. Do not gate the new assignment
differently from the existing pc_plus_imm assignment — it
is the same operand flow with one extra (smaller) adder.

Header comment update: note that pc_plus_4 is computed in
ID alongside pc_plus_imm to keep the EX-stage adder count
minimal; latency unchanged (still 1-cycle into the ID/EX
register).

Replace the three `(in.pc + 32'd4)` expressions with
`in.pc_plus_4`. The mux SHAPES stay exactly the same; only
the operand source for two of the four pc_next mux inputs
and one of the two alu_result mux inputs changes.

In the always_ff EX/MEM register block:

1. pc_next mux (4:1 chain — same shape, two inputs
   re-sourced):

     reg_q.pc_next <= misalign_fault     ? in.pc_plus_4
                   : in.ctrl.is_jump   ? jump_target
                   : branch_taken      ? branch_target
                                       : in.pc_plus_4;

   (was: misalign_fault ? (in.pc + 32'd4) ... : (in.pc + 32'd4))

2. alu_result mux for JAL link (2:1 — same shape, one input
   re-sourced):

     reg_q.alu_result <= in.ctrl.is_jump
                          ? in.pc_plus_4
                          : alu_result;

   (was: is_jump ? (in.pc + 32'd4) : alu_result)

Behavior table (no architectural change):

  | Retired instr     | pc_next         | alu_result          |
  |-------------------|-----------------|---------------------|
  | branch (taken)    | branch_target   | alu_result (regular)|
  | branch (not)      | in.pc_plus_4    | alu_result (regular)|
  | JAL               | jump_target     | in.pc_plus_4 (link) |
  | JALR              | jump_target     | in.pc_plus_4 (link) |
  | misalign trap     | in.pc_plus_4    | (reg_write=0)       |
  | other (ALU op)    | in.pc_plus_4    | alu_result (regular)|

Every column is bit-identical to today: in.pc_plus_4 carries
the same 32-bit value as `in.pc + 32'd4` would. branch_target,
jump_target, and `alu_result` (regular ALU output) are
UNCHANGED.

Do NOT change:
  - branch_cond, branch_taken, branch_op decode: UNCHANGED.
  - jalr_sum = rs1 + in.imm (depends on forwarded rs1; cannot
    be lifted to ID): UNCHANGED.
  - jalr_target = {jalr_sum[31:1], 1'b0}: UNCHANGED.
  - branch_target = in.pc_plus_imm (hyp-008 alias): UNCHANGED.
  - jump_target = in.ctrl.is_jalr ? jalr_target : in.pc_plus_imm:
    UNCHANGED.
  - misalign_branch / misalign_jump / misalign_fault:
    UNCHANGED. (These inspect the same bits of the same
    values; pc_plus_4 is not on these paths.)
  - ctrl_with_trap (is_illegal/reg_write zero-out on trap):
    UNCHANGED.
  - redirect formula:
      redirect = (branch_taken || in.ctrl.is_jump) && !misalign_fault
    UNCHANGED. THIS IS THE FORMULA hyp-004/005 MODIFIED AND
    BROKE. Leaving it alone is the correctness anchor.
  - redirect_target = in.ctrl.is_jump ? jump_target : branch_target:
    UNCHANGED. (hyp-009 tried to collapse this and lost Fmax;
    leave it alone.)
  - EX/MEM register layout (no new fields, no rename of
    existing fields).
  - reg_q.write_data, reg_q.branch_taken, reg_q.branch_target,
    reg_q.rs?_val, reg_q.rs?_addr, reg_q.rd, reg_q.instr,
    reg_q.valid, reg_q.ctrl: ALL UNCHANGED.
  - Divider handshake (start_div, div_busy, div_done,
    div_started_q, div_completed_q, div_pipeline_stall,
    ex_capture): UNCHANGED.
  - Forwarding muxes for rs1/rs2 from EX/MEM and MEM/WB:
    UNCHANGED.

Header comment update: note that the JAL link value (pc+4
written to rd) and the sequential / misalign-fallback
next-PC are now sourced from the ID-stage-precomputed
pc_plus_4 field, eliminating the +4 incrementer from the
EX/MEM register input cone. Latency unchanged. RVFI fields
(pc_wdata, rd_wdata for JAL) capture identical values to
today.

implementation_notes:
# hyp-20260427-010 — Pre-compute PC+4 in ID

## What changed

Three files, exactly as the hypothesis prescribed:

### `rtl/core_pkg.sv`
Added a single `logic [31:0] pc_plus_4` field at the **end** of `id_ex_t`,
immediately after `pc_plus_imm`. No changes to any other struct
(`if_id_t`, `ex_mem_t`, `mem_wb_t`, `ctrl_t`). Header comment extended
to describe the field's semantics (value-equivalent precompute, same
discipline as `pc_plus_imm`).

### `rtl/id_stage.sv`
- Added `logic [31:0] pc_plus_4_w;` and `always_comb pc_plus_4_w = in.pc + 32'd4;`
  immediately after the existing `pc_plus_imm_w` precompute.
- Added `reg_q.pc_plus_4 <= pc_plus_4_w;` in the existing
  `!(reset || flush) && !stall` capture branch, beside
  `reg_q.pc_plus_imm <= pc_plus_imm_w;`.
- Header comment updated to mention the new precompute and that the
  ID critical path is unchanged (the +4 adder runs in parallel with
  decoder / regfile-read / imm_gen / pc_plus_imm).

### `rtl/ex_stage.sv`
Replaced the three `(in.pc + 32'd4)` expressions with `in.pc_plus_4`:

1. `reg_q.alu_result` JAL-link mux (the 2:1 shape preserved).
2. `reg_q.pc_next` misalign-trap fallback (4:1 mux input 0).
3. `reg_q.pc_next` sequential default (4:1 mux input 3).

Mux shapes are byte-identical; only the operand source for those three
inputs changed. Header comment extended with a short note that the JAL
link value and sequential / misalign-fallback next-PC now source from
`in.pc_plus_4` (eliminating the +4 incrementer from the EX/MEM register
input cone).

Nothing else in `ex_stage.sv` was touched: branch_cond / branch_taken,
jalr_sum / jalr_target, branch_target / jump_target, misalign_*
trap detection, `ctrl_with_trap`, the `redirect` formula, and
`redirect_target` are all unchanged. The divider handshake
(`start_div_w`, `div_started_q`, `div_completed_q`,
`div_pipeline_stall`, `ex_capture`) is untouched. Forwarding muxes
are untouched.

## Files NOT modified
`rtl/if_stage.sv`, `rtl/decoder.sv`, `rtl/imm_gen.sv`, `rtl/alu.sv`,
`rtl/divu.sv`, `rtl/mem_stage.sv`, `rtl/wb_stage.sv`,
`rtl/forward_unit.sv`, `rtl/hazard_unit.sv`, `rtl/reg_file.sv`,
`rtl/core.sv`. Top-level wiring is unchanged because the new field
lives entirely inside the ID/EX bundle that flows through
`id_stage` → `ex_stage`.

## Deviations from the plan
None of substance. The only judgment calls were prose:

- The hypothesis suggested a struct header comment for `pc_plus_4`. I
  appended it to the existing `id_ex_t` doc-block (continuing the
  same paragraph style hyp-008 introduced for `pc_plus_imm`) rather
  than introducing a separate block — same content, lower diff churn.
- The hypothesis suggested updating the `ex_stage` and `id_stage`
  module headers; I did so, but kept the additions to one short
  paragraph each so the headers stay scannable.

## Concerns

1. **Yosys may already share the +4 adder.** If it does, the
   structural impact on the post-synth netlist is near zero and
   fitness will land at hyp-008's 373.66 ± noise. The hypothesis
   acknowledges this (target is +1-2% with downside bounded near
   neutral). No way to predict from the SystemVerilog source alone.

2. **EX critical path may not be Fmax-binding after hyp-008.** If
   the binding net moved to the IF cone or the regfile-read in ID
   after hyp-008's win, this change will be a no-op for Fmax. Same
   bounded-downside argument applies.

3. **+32 FFs.** Confirmed harmless: ~1.6% of GW2AR-LV's ~10K FF
   budget, well below any utilization concern.

4. **No new RVFI surface.** `pc_plus_4` is consumed only by the
   EX/MEM register-Q input. It does not appear in any RVFI field
   (which all flow from MEM/WB). pc_wdata = `mem_wb_w.pc_next` and
   rd_wdata for JAL = `mem_wb_w.alu_result` are both
   value-equivalent to the prior implementation: same 32-bit sum
   on the same registered values, computed one cycle earlier.

## Verification
`verilator --lint-only -Wall -Wno-MULTITOP -sv rtl/*.sv` passes clean
(no errors, no warnings).
